pattern: Once the array is sorted, every element is the maximum of a subsequence exactly when its companions are drawn from the smaller elements before it, and the minimum of a subsequence exactly when its companions are drawn from the larger elements after it, so counting those companion choices with binomial coefficients turns an exponential enumeration into a linear scan.
hints: Sort the array first. In sorted order, an element can only be the maximum of a subsequence if every other member of that subsequence sits to its left, and only be the minimum if every other member sits to its right.

For the element at sorted position i, the number of at-most-k-sized subsequences where it is the maximum equals the number of ways to pick 0, 1, ..., up to k-1 companions from the i elements before it -- a sum of binomial coefficients. The minimum case is the mirror image using the elements after it.
Precompute factorials and modular inverse factorials so each binomial coefficient is O(1), then sum each element times (its max-count plus its min-count) over the whole array, modulo 1e9+7.

edge_inputs.description: single element, only one subsequence exists
two elements with k covering both
k equal to 1, every subsequence is a single element
k equal to the array length, covers every subsequence up to the full array
all zero values at the minimum bound
already-sorted input so max/min positions are not shuffled by the sort step
reverse-sorted input to exercise the sort step

stress.description: random arrays with a random k bounded by 70 and the array length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 20000)))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, min(70, n))
    return [nums, k]
